# 2026-03-31 23:30:43Z

<!-- cursoride Session 1503e77e-692a-479b-af8c-ebe1ebd86b52 (2026-03-31 23:30:43Z) -->

_**User**_

This project right now is an incremental clicker game where you accumulate coins by clicking on a coin, and using those coins to buy generators (they are incremental, higher cost but gives higher production rate in return) in the form of furniture in the room your in. I want to change this into a game where you click a piece of scrap (replacing the coin) to earn scrap to buy generators in the form of robots that mine the mountain of scrap in the distance. When a robot/generator is bought it should spawn a robot on the mountain (fulfilling this aim: Eases for Planting Generators (3 points): the player can deploy generators (that add to the resource rate but cost some of the resource count) through an XR interaction. This XR interaction triggers an animation that uses motion eases on a visual element that tracks with the number of generators planted.).

There should already be pre-existing code in place which should make implementing this change easy

---

_**Agent (claude-4.6-sonnet-medium-thinking)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me plan the changes needed:

1. **`CoinClicker.cs`** → Rename/rebrand to `ScrapClicker.cs` (or modify `CoinClicker.cs` to work with scrap)
   - Change "Click me!" text to something scrap-related
   - Keep the same `OnClick` logic (it calls `updateCoins`)

2. **`resource.cs`** → Update display text from "Coins" to "Scrap"
   - Change `resourceText.text` from "Coins: ..." to "Scrap: ..."
   - Update the secondary resource display from "Joy" to something else like "Metal"
   - The scrap thresholds can remain as-is or be adjusted if needed

3. **`shop.cs`** → Add robot spawning animation with easing
   - When a robot/generator is bought, animate it spawning with motion eases
   - Track robot count and apply visual effect

4. **`ShopUI.cs`** → Update "c" (coins) suffix to "s" (scrap) suffix

5. **New script: `RobotSpawner.cs`** →
   - When BuyItem is called, spawn the robot prefab with an eased animation
   - Create a visual counter or progress bar that animates with motion easing whenever a new generator is planted, fulfilling the assignment requirement for eased animations tied to generator deployment

I'll need to update the text labels across a few scripts (changing "Coins" to "Scrap", adjusting thresholds, and updating the cost display suffix), then implement the core animation logic either in a dedicated counter script or directly in the resource manager to handle the eased counter updates whenever a robot is purchased. I'm going through the specific text replacements needed across the codebase—swapping "Coins" for "Scrap" in the resource display, updating the clicker button text, and changing the shop UI suffix from coins to scrap. Then I'll wire up the robot count display to track total deployments and trigger an eased animation whenever a new robot is purchased, using a coroutine that scales the display element smoothly.

Writing the RobotCountDisplay component... Still writing animation logic...

Actually, let me reconsider the easing approach here. The requirement is that the visual element tracking robot count should animate with proper easing when the count changes, not just linearly. I think using an AnimationCurve would be the cleanest way to handle this—it gives me flexible control over the easing behavior.

My strategy: apply the easing curve to the time parameter itself, then use a sine wave to create the punch effect. So as time progresses from 0 to 1, I sample the AnimationCurve to get an eased time value, then feed that into a sine function to create the scale-up-and-back-down motion. This way the easing controls the pacing of the punch, whether it's linear, ease-out, or something more complex.

Now for the shop integration—I need to wire up the robot count display. The total robot count should be the sum of all purchased items across the shop, so I'll add a reference to `RobotCountDisplay` in `shop.cs` and update it whenever an item is bought.

I'm also reconsidering the currency label change. Since we're renaming "Coins" to "Scrap" throughout, I should update the "c" suffix in `ShopUI.cs` to "s" for consistency, though I want to make sure that aligns with what the assignment expects.

Let me start implementing these changes across all the files. the class name `CoinClicker` but just update the display strings. The user wants minimal changes, so I'll focus on cosmetic updates: renaming "Coin" to "Scrap" in the UI text, changing "Furniture generators" to "Robot generators" through the Unity inspector, and updating the cost label from "c" to "s". I also need to add robot spawn animations and track robot counts in the shop logic.

Now I'm implementing these changes across the resource and UI scripts.</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-4.6-sonnet-medium-thinking)**_

Everything looks good. Here's a summary of all the changes made and what you need to do in the Unity Editor to complete the transition:

---

## Code Changes Made

### Modified files

**`resource.cs`** — Display text now reads `"Scrap: ..."` instead of `"Coins: ..."`, and the Joy hint mentions robots instead of furniture.

**`CoinClicker.cs`** — The idle prompt on the clickable object now says `"Grab Scrap!"` instead of `"Click me!"`.

**`ShopUI.cs`** — Buy/upgrade cost labels now use `"s"` (scrap) instead of `"c"` (coins) as the unit suffix.

**`shop.cs`** — Added a public `RobotCountDisplay robotCountDisplay` field. Every time `BuyItem()` successfully completes, it calls `robotCountDisplay.AddRobot()` to trigger the animation.

### New file: `RobotCountDisplay.cs`

This is the core of the assignment requirement. It:
- Has a `TMP_Text countText` that displays `"N Robots Deployed"`
- Has a public `AnimationCurve scaleCurve` (configurable in Inspector) — this is your motion ease
- When `AddRobot()` is called, runs a coroutine (`PunchScaleEased`) that animates the text with a **sin-envelope punch scaled by the ease curve** — so the motion is never linear, always eased

---

## Unity Editor Setup Required

1. **Swap the clickable object**: Replace the `Coin_Circle` mesh with a scrap/junk part mesh from `Assets/Junk Car Parts/`. The `XRSimpleInteractable → CoinClicker.OnClick` wiring stays the same.

2. **Swap generator prefabs**: In the `GameMaster` inspector, replace the furniture prefabs in each `ShopItem.prefab` slot with `robotSphere.prefab` (from `Assets/RobotSphere/Assets/`). Update the `spawnPoints` transforms to positions on/near the mountain of scrap in your scene.

3. **Add the RobotCountDisplay**: 
   - Create a world-space Canvas or a TMP_Text object near the mountain
   - Attach the `RobotCountDisplay` component to a GameObject
   - Assign the `TMP_Text` to its `countText` field
   - Optionally tune the `scaleCurve` in the Inspector (default is ease-in-out, but you can make it a custom bounce)

4. **Wire it to shop**: On the `GameMaster` object, assign the `RobotCountDisplay` component into the new `Robot Count Display` field in the `shop` component.

---

_**User**_
